seed: vulnerability vs strength; symmetry_vs_break; cross_current; braid; flood against stack; pressure_border; fast_vs_slow; tiny_face; desire_emptiness | abstract variation request 125; target batch count 500
title: Exposure vs Shelter
reasoning: This composition expresses the emotional tension between vulnerability and strength through contrasting colors and patterns that reflect the themes of exposure and shelter. The palette of signal green, electric blue, dead black, static gray, and hot magenta is chosen for its dynamic interplay, contrasting vibrancy against muted tones, embodying the idea of strong presence versus soft vulnerability. The main collision occurs in the center, where the vibrant electric blue disrupts the surrounding quieter static gray, signifying a strong, exposed core amidst uncertain edges. The layout of horizontal and vertical movements adds a sense of tension and conflict to the piece, evoking waves of emotional currents. Broken pixels are strategically placed to introduce imperfection and suggest that strength is often a defense against the rawness of vulnerability.

layout.name: cross_current
layout.description: Horizontal and vertical movements collide, creating a tense crossing in the middle.

composition.name: exposure_vs_shelter
composition.idea: A pale exposed center is either protected by a frame or left open to incoming marks.

referenceExample.title: Lonely Signal
referenceExample.pattern: single bright dot with faint surrounding echo

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
